title: constants.ts — EMAIL_REGEX restricted to .com domains only
commit_message: fix: align EMAIL_REGEX with product requirement (commercial TLD only)

why_email_cascade.explanation: Same cascade pattern as scenario 2 (single-regex change in constants.ts): CRITICAL = describe('EMAIL_REGEX'); HIGH = every other regex describe in standalone/regex.constants.test.js plus cross-dependent rows whose sources[] include constants.ts.
why_email_cascade.key_point: 3 CRITICAL + 23 HIGH + 51 SKIP = 77. Mirrors scenario_2_expected_output.json structure with EMAIL_REGEX as the CRITICAL symbol instead of PHONE_REGEX.

CRITICAL.reason: describe('EMAIL_REGEX') label exactly matches changed symbol

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > accepts a valid .com email address
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects an email address missing the @ symbol
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects a non-.com domain (e.g. .org)
tests.result: ⚠️  REVIEW — contract now rejects all non-.com TLDs; assertion text may need to match new rule

HIGH.reason: Same source file (constants.ts) — sibling regex describes; plus cross-dependent rows with constants.ts (EMAIL_REGEX excluded here — it is CRITICAL only)

tests.test: PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > accepts a password meeting all requirements
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without an uppercase letter
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without a special character
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password longer than 30 characters
CARD_REGEX > accepts a string of digits and spaces
CARD_REGEX > rejects letters inside a card number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a 10-digit phone number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a phone number with a + country prefix
PHONE_REGEX > rejects a phone number shorter than 10 digits
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > accepts a valid alphanumeric username with a letter
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > rejects a username made entirely of digits
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > accepts a properly spaced 19-character card number
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber preserves first and last 4 digits
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > accepts a standard 19-character card number with spaces
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number that contains letters

SKIP.reason: sources[] have zero overlap with src/types/constants.ts
skipped_files: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js (isUserLoggedIn, updateNewTokenDetails, capitalizeFirstLetter describes — constants.ts not in sources[])
test_repository/cross-dependent/payment-state.cross.test.js (updateUserCards, updatePaymentPlanDetails, CLEARCARDS describes — constants.ts not in sources[])

coverage_gaps.detail: International TLDs are rejected by the new pattern; update UX copy and any fixtures that used .io / .org addresses.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "EMAIL_REGEX|PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD|CARD_REGEX|PHONE_REGEX|UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX|validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|storageKeys align with payment flow|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout" --verbose
